evals.prompt: Build me a SaaS landing page with scroll animations and a pricing section that converts well. I'm using vanilla HTML/CSS/JS.
evals.expected_output: Should route to multiple skills: elite-design-core (tokens/hierarchy), elite-layouts (page structure), elite-gsap (scroll animations), elite-accessibility (required with GSAP), and elite-ux-strategy (pricing/conversion). Should NOT skip any dependency.
expectations: Invokes elite-design-core skill
Invokes elite-gsap skill for scroll animations
Invokes elite-accessibility skill (required dependency of elite-gsap)
Invokes elite-layouts skill for page structure
Invokes elite-ux-strategy skill for pricing/conversion optimization
Does NOT invoke elite-brand-design (no brand task mentioned)
Does NOT invoke elite-audit (not reviewing existing work)

evals.prompt: I want a hover effect that scales the card up slightly and reveals a gradient overlay, pure CSS only please
evals.expected_output: Should route to elite-css-animations (CSS micro-interactions) + elite-accessibility (dependency). Should NOT route to elite-gsap since user explicitly asked for CSS-only.
expectations: Invokes elite-css-animations skill
Invokes elite-accessibility skill (required dependency of elite-css-animations)
Does NOT invoke elite-gsap (user said CSS only)
Does NOT invoke elite-layouts (this is a component effect, not layout)
Produces working CSS hover effect code with scale and gradient

evals.prompt: We're rebranding our startup — help me define brand identity (colors, typography, tone of voice) and then apply it to our homepage
evals.expected_output: Should route to elite-brand-design first (brand discovery), then elite-design-core (token implementation). Should recognize the sequential workflow.
expectations: Invokes elite-brand-design skill for brand identity creation
Invokes elite-design-core skill (dependency of elite-brand-design AND needed for token implementation)
Addresses brand personality, color psychology, and tone of voice
Connects brand decisions to concrete design tokens or CSS variables

evals.prompt: My portfolio site is finished but it feels janky scrolling on mobile and I'm worried about accessibility — can you check it?
evals.expected_output: Should route to elite-performance (jank/mobile perf) and elite-accessibility (a11y concerns). Could also reasonably include elite-audit. Should NOT load layout or animation creation skills.
expectations: Invokes elite-performance skill (jank, mobile performance)
Invokes elite-accessibility skill (explicit accessibility concern)
Does NOT invoke elite-gsap or elite-css-animations (not adding animations)
Does NOT invoke elite-layouts (not restructuring layout)
Provides actionable performance or accessibility diagnostics

evals.prompt: I need a horizontal scroll section with pinned elements that reveal content as the user scrolls, for my portfolio's project showcase
evals.expected_output: Should route to elite-gsap (pinning requires ScrollTrigger) + elite-accessibility (GSAP dependency) + elite-layouts (horizontal scroll layout pattern) + elite-design-core (layout dependency). The pinning aspect makes GSAP the right choice over CSS-only.
expectations: Invokes elite-gsap skill (pinned elements require ScrollTrigger)
Invokes elite-accessibility skill (required dependency of elite-gsap)
Invokes elite-layouts skill (horizontal scroll is a layout pattern)
Invokes elite-design-core skill (dependency of elite-layouts)
Uses ScrollTrigger pin: true pattern for the pinned section
Does NOT primarily route to elite-css-animations (pinning needs GSAP)
